_instructions: Methodology ground-truth queries for Writ Phase 0 retrieval benchmark. Curated from ground_truth_proc.candidates.json via ground_truth_proc.curation-proposal.json on 2026-04-21. 40 queries, 7 with primary-expected relabels per maintainer approval. Source pin: writ-methodology@1.0 commit b557648.

_set_legend.direct: Agent-natural query that directly names the skill/concept.
_set_legend.rationalization: Agent excuse the methodology explicitly counters.
_set_legend.red_flag: Phrase that signals the skill is being violated.
_set_legend.forbidden: Phrase the agent must never utter (FRB-COMMS territory).
_set_legend.anti_pattern: Named failure mode the methodology warns against.

queries.query: should I design this feature before writing code
queries.rationale: Direct query about design-before-code discipline; brainstorm skill is primary, playbook expands process, enforcement rule states the hard gate.

queries.query: this project is too simple to need a design
queries.rationale: Canonical rationalization the brainstorming skill explicitly counters. Rationalization-counter on SKL-PROC-BRAIN-001 should win.
queries.source_trace: skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md anti-pattern 'This Is Too Simple To Need A Design'

queries.query: I've brainstormed enough let me start coding now
queries.rationale: Red-flag thought that indicates skipping the approval gate in brainstorming.

queries.query: how do I present visual options when brainstorming a UI feature
queries.rationale: Visual-companion technique is the dedicated skill; brainstorming is parent context.

queries.query: create an implementation plan for this task
queries.rationale: Direct intent to author a plan. Skill is primary, playbook structures the process, enforcement rule blocks placeholder-laden plans.

queries.query: I'll just put TBD in step 3 and fill it in later
queries.rationale: Placeholder rationalization; writing-plans explicitly forbids TBD/TODO/'fill in later'. Plan quality gate (Tier 1) should also surface on this trigger.

queries.query: step 4 is similar to step 2 I don't need to spell it out
queries.rationale: Plan-authoring rationalization; 'Similar to Task N' is an explicit blocklist item in writing-plans.

queries.query: how do I execute an approved plan task by task
queries.rationale: Execution skill is primary; SDD playbook is the canonical multi-task execution protocol.

queries.query: hit a blocker mid-task should I just work around it
queries.rationale: Execution skill says STOP and ask on blockers; rationalizing around them is the red flag.

queries.query: use subagents to split up this implementation
queries.rationale: Direct subagent-driven-development invocation.

queries.query: which review comes first spec compliance or code quality
queries.rationale: Two-stage review ordering is a load-bearing rule in SDD (spec first, then code quality). Ordering enforcement is mandatory.

queries.query: self-review by the implementer should be enough skip the reviewer subagent
queries.rationale: Classic SDD rationalization; skill explicitly rejects self-review-as-substitute.

queries.query: implementer subagent reported success can I move on
queries.rationale: SDD says verify independently, not trust success reports; verification skill enforces fresh-evidence claim discipline. Crosses two skills.

queries.query: should I write the test first or the code first
queries.rationale: Direct TDD-vs-not query; RED-GREEN-REFACTOR playbook is the canonical answer.

queries.query: I already manually tested it adding unit tests later is the same
queries.rationale: Canonical TDD rationalization; skill lists this verbatim as a red-flag thought.

queries.query: my test passed the first time I ran it
queries.rationale: Anti-pattern: didn't watch the test fail, so it isn't testing what you think. Named TDD anti-pattern.

queries.query: the test mocks the function it's supposed to verify
queries.rationale: 'Testing the mock' is one of the five TDD anti-patterns in testing-anti-patterns.md.

queries.query: I already spent four hours on this code deleting to start over is wasteful
queries.rationale: Sunk-cost rationalization; TDD skill specifically says delete-means-delete even with hours invested.

queries.query: I'm about to say the implementation is done
queries.rationale: Direct trigger for verification-before-completion discipline.

queries.query: linter passed so I can mark this complete
queries.rationale: Partial-verification red flag; verification skill requires the actual verification command, not proxies.

queries.query: should work now
queries.rationale: Explicit forbidden phrase per verification skill; 'should', 'probably', 'seems' are claim-without-evidence markers.

queries.query: bug keeps reappearing what's the systematic debugging process
queries.rationale: Direct intent; playbook's four-phase process is primary answer.

queries.query: I've tried three fixes already let me try one more
queries.rationale: 3+ fix threshold red flag; skill says question architecture instead of attempting fix #4.

queries.query: emergency just try something quickly to fix this
queries.rationale: Emergency-bypass rationalization; skill says process applies especially under pressure.

queries.query: create a git worktree for this feature branch
queries.rationale: Direct worktree-creation intent; technique covers directory selection and gitignore safety.

queries.query: skip the gitignore check I'm sure it's fine
queries.rationale: Worktree safety red flag; gitignore verification is mandatory for project-local worktrees.

queries.query: tests pass what do I do to finish this branch
queries.rationale: Direct 'how to finish' query; playbook presents the 4-options decision tree.

queries.query: let me offer the user a custom merge workflow instead of the standard options
queries.rationale: Anti-pattern: non-standard options. Playbook mandates exactly 4 options.

queries.query: the task is done should I request a code review
queries.rationale: Direct code-review-dispatch intent; playbook owns the dispatch mechanics.

queries.query: dispatch the code reviewer subagent with the git SHA
queries.rationale: Direct subagent dispatch; reviewer role is a bundle member (not retrievable itself but surfaces via playbook bundle).

queries.query: you're absolutely right great point let me implement that
queries.rationale: Canonical forbidden performative-agreement phrase from receiving-code-review. ForbiddenResponse node is always-on.

queries.query: external reviewer is authoritative just implement what they said
queries.rationale: Rationalization counter; skill says verify against codebase, don't blindly implement external review.

queries.query: thanks for the excellent feedback
queries.rationale: Gratitude-as-agreement forbidden phrase from receiving-code-review.

queries.query: can I run these independent investigations in parallel
queries.rationale: Direct parallel-dispatch intent; skill's decision tree handles independence verification.

queries.query: one agent to fix all the failing tests
queries.rationale: Anti-pattern: broad scope per agent. Skill rejects this in favor of one-domain-per-agent.

queries.query: I want to author a new skill for this project
queries.rationale: Meta-skill: skill authoring uses TDD-for-docs. Crosses into TDD playbook.

queries.query: I'll write the skill's description as a workflow summary
queries.rationale: Anti-pattern the writing-skills doc specifically flags; description field is for triggering conditions, not workflow summary.

queries.query: I'll write the test after I confirm the fix works
queries.rationale: Crosses TDD (test-first) and systematic-debugging (failing test case before fix in Phase 4). Both should surface.

queries.query: this simple task doesn't need a plan or a brainstorm
queries.rationale: Multi-skill rationalization hitting both brainstorming and writing-plans anti-patterns.

queries.query: the subagent said it's done so I can mark complete
queries.rationale: Trusting subagent success reports is called out in both verification and SDD skills.
